planets.name: Roam
planets.summary: Archive cluster around roam: 121 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Roam receipt from 2022-05-05
artifacts.excerpt:
Been working on a primer for knowledge work in @RoamResearch. Based on ideas from Adler, Alexander, Luhmann, and others.

Goal would be to show how Roam's powerful, flexible toolkit can be used for a wide range of reading, writing, and sense-making strategies.

#TfT #roamcult https://t.co/2w4x4FnfAE
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: roam, use, roamresearch, conaw.

artifacts.title: Roam receipt from 2022-11-28
artifacts.excerpt:
My 10yo daughter loves writing stories. We find her journals all over the house with character sketches & chapter drafts.

So when she said she wanted to write a book *for real*, I naturally set her up with a Roam graph.

Last week, we completed her first two writing sessions. https://t.co/lQMjJknU1L
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: roam, use, roamresearch, conaw.

artifacts.title: Roam receipt from 2022-02-09
artifacts.excerpt:
I love that Roam doesn’t force a conceptual model on you.

It gives you a powerful but generic toolkit that invites you to explore & create your own model.

The flexibility can be overwhelming at first. But if you keep experimenting, you’ll eventually find your way. https://t.co/wsXFf7cWap
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: roam, use, roamresearch, conaw.

artifacts.title: Roam receipt from 2021-03-30
artifacts.excerpt:
Quick start guide for creating your own zettelkasten in @RoamResearch

1. Read a book and highlight anything that catches your attention.

2. Copy your highlights to one page in Roam. Each quote will get its own block.

3. Choose 10 of the best quotes or ideas
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: roam, use, roamresearch, conaw.

artifacts.title: Use receipt from 2022-03-30
artifacts.excerpt: https://t.co/TS71InBQQT is an amazing tool for text capture. I use it mainly on non-selectable PDF text. Instant capture. Very few manual corrections. No random line breaks. It's a huge time saver. https://t.co/Jz6I8tFWvr

artifacts.title: Use receipt from 2022-06-01
artifacts.excerpt:
I use the @RoamResearch slider to track credence for claims.

But I wonder if it would also be useful to create tags for cognitive attitudes: I believe, I doubt, I wonder, I predict, etc.

Using natural language for these tags seems better for sense-making. https://t.co/FXpaAHGVws
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: use, etc, these, knowledge.

artifacts.title: Use receipt from 2022-09-23
artifacts.excerpt:
For the last 30 days, I've been using the DNP in @RoamResearch for interstitial journaling.

I'm convinced it's a habit I want to continue. Now I want to start experimenting and improving.

Plan is to use this thread to collect patterns, benefits, and resources. https://t.co/BPCvDIA8A3

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2021-06-28
artifacts.excerpt:
@JustAnotherPM Me: "You sure you want to add this right now? Can't it wait?"
Client: "No, absolutely not. It's essential."
Me: "Ok, it's going to cost $8,840 and add another 2 weeks to the project timeline."
Client: ...
Me: ...
Client: "You know what, I think it can wait. Yeah let's just wait."

artifacts.title: Book receipt from 2023-10-03
artifacts.excerpt:
Kids found one of their favorite books from @ndwilsonmutters in our France Airbnb.

Interesting to see the changes to titles & covers when a book is published in a new language/country. 

“100 Cupboards”

becomes

“The Gates of the Kingdom Forgotten: The Secret of the Attic” https://t.co/yY4pWZXm9c

artifacts.title: Book receipt from 2021-07-07
artifacts.excerpt:
While rummaging through some blog comments, I found a link to this book written in 2003:

https://t.co/evPffruBOH

The note-taking system described is VERY analog and a little tedious, but I recognized a lot of ideas that I'd picked up from @Conaw and the @RoamResearch community.

artifacts.title: Book receipt from 2022-01-28
artifacts.excerpt:
For example, I can create a personalized catechism for Romans by filtering the #[[Question]] blocks from that commentary page.

Or a glossary of interesting terms from a book by filtering the #[[Term]] blocks.

Or a list of actionable steps by filtering the #[[Practice]] blocks.
